div1.entryFree: Pitch, subst. height: when from highmost p. he (the sun) reeleth, Sonn. 7, 9. to write above a mortal p. 86, 6. of what validity and p. soe'er, Tw. I, 1, 12. it (the frame) is of such a spacious lofty p. H6A II, 3, 55. the p. and height of all his thoughts, R3 III, 7, 188. and mount her p. Tit. II, 1, 14. I cannot bound a p. above dull woe, Rom. I, 4, 21. enterprises of great p. and moment, Hml. III, 1, 86 (Ff pith). Used of the height to which a falcon soars: which flies the higher p. H6A II, 4, 11. what a p. she flew, H6B II, 1, 6. bears his thoughts above his falcon's p. H6B II, 1, 6 Figuratively: how high a p. his resolution soars, R2 I, 1, 109. fly an ordinary p. Caes. I, 1, 78. Uncertain which of the two homonyms is meant: this imperious man will work us all from princes into pages: all men's honours lie like one lump before him, to be fashioned into what p. he please, H8 II, 2, 50 (height? or baseness? Hanmer pinch, Theobald batch).
